original_abstract: Visual Question Answering (VQA) involves extracting relevant information from visual data to provide accurate natural language responses. While VQA holds significant potential for applications such as robotic interaction, automated surveillance, and assistive technologies, achieving high accuracy remains a challenge. Current systems often struggle because image features fail to capture complex scene dynamics, and textual representations frequently lack sufficient depth. To address these limitations, this paper proposes a dual-pronged approach leveraging multi-scale feature extraction and fusion for both visual and linguistic components. First, we enhance image characterization by extracting features from multiple layers of a pre-trained deep neural network, identifying the optimal fusion strategy through rigorous experimentation. Second, we introduce a multi-scale textual representation that integrates word-, phrase-, and sentence-level features to more effectively capture semantic nuance. By incorporating these multi-scale mechanisms into the VQA architecture, we demonstrate a measurable improvement in model performance, highlighting the effectiveness of multi-layered feature integration in bridging the gap between vision and language.
humanized_abstract: Visual Question Answering (VQA) is a field of study which deals with extracting answers from images by using a meaningful representation of the output in the form of natural language. VQA not only has tremendous scope in the area of robotic interaction, video surveillance as well as assistive technologies but also faces a major bottleneck in the form of low accuracy achieved by state of the art models. Typically, there are two main reasons because of which VQA models suffer in achieving accuracy - the image features are unable to capture the dynamics of a scene and on the other hand the text features are not capable of conveying the required amount of information. In this paper, we try to overcome these two major hindrances in the VQA models. For this, we come up with an enhanced version of VQA model by proposing a method which has two main components: i) A model to extract multi-scale deep features and fuse them optimally for images and ii) Multi-scale representation for texts at word, phrase and sentence levels. Through experiments on various datasets, we confirm that achieving enhanced performance in VQA can be done by using multi-scale deep feature fusion for the enhanced image and textual features.
